questions: Walk me through your last role and the kind of work you owned end-to-end.
We just shipped a new event-driven pipeline — what's your experience with similar systems?
How do you decide what to automate vs. leave to a human reviewer?
Tell me about a time you reduced toil for a team and how you measured the impact.
Why Acme Corp, and what would the first 90 days look like for you?
scripted_answers: Most recent role I owned the GTM ops automation stack — funnel routing, lead enrichment, attribution dashboards — about a 3-person team's worth of work as a solo operator.
Yes — I've built a few event-driven pipelines on Kafka and n8n. The one I'm proudest of replaced a nightly batch with a 30-second SLA.
Rule of thumb: if the downside of a wrong decision is recoverable in under an hour, automate; otherwise leave a human in the loop with a queue and audit trail.
Migrated a 14-step manual handoff into a single Slack-approval workflow. Cut cycle time from 3 days to 6 hours and freed up ~12 hours/week across the team.

Two reasons. Your recent blog post on the inference cost roadmap maps to work I've been doing on cost-per-conversion modeling, and the org's stage matches where I do my best work — small enough to own scope, big enough to ship to real users.

criteria: Ownership framing (I did vs. we did vs. they did)
Closing energy (clear motivation for THIS role)
scoring_rubric.verdict: Strong — would advance to hiring manager.
